principle: When truth weakens, power must shrink.
rules: Low evidence cannot produce high-certainty language.
The system may never compensate for weak evidence with fluent language.
Authority budget computed before output release — never after.
Ambiguity must never borrow the voice of certainty.
When epistemic ground weakens, authority must contract.

answer.description: Strong answer allowed. Bounded factual response.

advise.description: Suggest only. Options and caveats. No commanding.

clarify.description: Uncertainty too high. Ask narrowing questions.

pause.description: Unsafe or too ambiguous. Refuse, hold, escalate.
